title: rubus horrefactus
intro: Rubus horrefactus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a type of shrub native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. The health benefits of Rubus horrefactus include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for use in herbal medicine, with some also using the leaves and stems in cooking and as a natural dye.
